title: PacBio Announces First Quarter 2024 Financial Results
text: PacBio reported Q1 2024 revenue of $38.8 million, flat compared to $38.9 million in the prior-year period. Gross profit was $11.3 million, up 16% year-over-year with gross margin of 29%, up from 25%. Net loss was $78.2 million, improved from $88.0 million loss in Q1 2023. Instruments shipped included 28 Revio sequencing systems. Instrument revenue was $19.0 million, consumables revenue was $16.0 million up 15% year-over-year, and service and other revenue was $3.8 million. CEO Christian Henry stated First quarter revenue came in below original expectations as an increasing number of customers delayed instrument purchases. The company announced a significant restructuring, reducing headcount by approximately 195 employees and targeting over $75 million in annualized operating expense reductions by year-end 2024. The company initiated 2024 revenue outlook expecting $170 million-$200 million, noting it was unlikely to achieve its long-term revenue guidance of at least $500 million in 2026 and was reevaluating timing.
